---
title: "scRNA Annotation Method Report: scANVI"
date: today
jupyter: python3
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-expand: 3
    toc-location: left
    html-math-method: katex
    embed-resources: true
    page-layout: full
    html-table-processing: none
    theme: lumen
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 5
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import sys

from IPython.display import Markdown, display
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

LIB_DIR = Path(".").resolve() / "lib"
if str(LIB_DIR) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(LIB_DIR))

from report_utils import format_table, load_report_context, package_versions_table, style_bar

ROOT = Path(".").resolve()
context = load_report_context(ROOT)
```

## Current Status

```{python}
display(
    Markdown(
        """
This is a **scaffold report** for a future Python-native **scANVI** backend.
It is included now to reserve a clean place for the eventual method-specific
report, but the current runtime does **not** train or evaluate scANVI yet.
"""
    )
)
```

```{python}
status_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"field": "method", "value": "scanvi"},
        {"field": "implemented_in_runtime", "value": "no"},
        {"field": "intended_role", "value": "reference mapping and semi-supervised annotation"},
        {"field": "selected_in_current_run", "value": "scanvi" in context["selected_methods"]},
    ]
)
format_table(status_df)
```

## Why scANVI Matters

`scANVI` is the strongest next Python backend for this template when the user
has a labeled reference and wants a batch-aware model rather than only a
lightweight classifier.

```{python}
fit_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"strength": "reference-aware annotation", "why_it_matters": "supports mapping query cells to labeled atlases"},
        {"strength": "batch-aware latent modeling", "why_it_matters": "useful when annotation and integration interact"},
        {"strength": "uncertainty handling", "why_it_matters": "helps avoid forcing weak label assignments"},
    ]
)
format_table(fit_df)
```

```{python}
strength_profile = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"dimension": "reference mapping", "score": 5},
        {"dimension": "batch awareness", "score": 5},
        {"dimension": "runtime simplicity", "score": 2},
        {"dimension": "interpretability", "score": 3},
        {"dimension": "scalability", "score": 4},
    ]
)
strength_plot = px.bar(
    strength_profile,
    x="dimension",
    y="score",
    title="Expected scANVI profile in this template",
    range_y=[0, 5.5],
)
style_bar(strength_plot, height=420)
```

## Paper And Documentation Takeaways

The scvi-tools documentation positions scANVI as a semi-supervised extension of
scVI for label transfer and reference mapping. In practice, that means this
backend is most compelling when the project has a meaningful labeled reference
and wants batch-aware latent modeling rather than only a lightweight classifier.

```{python}
takeaways_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"takeaway": "semi-supervised setup", "implication": "requires clear treatment of labeled versus unknown cells"},
        {"takeaway": "counts-aware modeling", "implication": "raw-count assumptions must be enforced in runtime validation"},
        {"takeaway": "reference mapping focus", "implication": "best when a curated labeled atlas is already available"},
        {"takeaway": "batch-aware latent space", "implication": "useful when annotation and integration are tightly linked"},
    ]
)
format_table(takeaways_df)
```

## When To Prefer scANVI

```{python}
preference_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"project_situation": "curated labeled reference is available", "recommendation": "strong fit"},
        {"project_situation": "annotation and batch correction are tightly linked", "recommendation": "strong fit"},
        {"project_situation": "small routine dataset with a good CellTypist model", "recommendation": "usually overkill"},
        {"project_situation": "raw counts are missing", "recommendation": "do not use until counts-aware input is restored"},
    ]
)
format_table(preference_df)
```

## Required Future Inputs

```{python}
planned_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"input": "reference_h5ad", "purpose": "labeled reference atlas"},
        {"input": "reference_label_key", "purpose": "known cell type labels in the reference"},
        {"input": "counts layer", "purpose": "raw-count input required by scvi-tools models"},
        {"input": "batch_key", "purpose": "batch-aware modeling and mapping"},
        {"input": "optional GPU", "purpose": "practical acceleration for larger datasets"},
    ]
)
format_table(planned_df)
```

## Planned Report Contents

```{python}
diagnostics_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"planned_section": "embedding review", "content": "latent-space UMAP colored by predicted and final labels"},
        {"planned_section": "confidence review", "content": "uncertainty summaries and low-confidence populations"},
        {"planned_section": "cluster agreement", "content": "comparison against other annotation methods"},
        {"planned_section": "reference coverage", "content": "cells that map poorly or remain unresolved"},
    ]
)
format_table(diagnostics_df)
```

## Planned Runtime Contract

```{python}
contract_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"contract_item": "input layer", "expected_behavior": "require raw counts and fail fast otherwise"},
        {"contract_item": "reference labels", "expected_behavior": "validate presence and track unknown/unlabeled category explicitly"},
        {"contract_item": "output embeddings", "expected_behavior": "store latent representation and downstream UMAP/neighbors keys clearly"},
        {"contract_item": "review outputs", "expected_behavior": "surface low-confidence or weakly mapped cells in overview tables"},
    ]
)
format_table(contract_df)
```

## Readiness Checklist

```{python}
checklist_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"task": "add runtime parameters", "status": "pending"},
        {"task": "require counts-aware input validation", "status": "pending"},
        {"task": "write scANVI-specific result tables", "status": "pending"},
        {"task": "add report figures and comparison outputs", "status": "pending"},
    ]
)
format_table(checklist_df)
```

```{python}
checklist_plot = px.bar(
    checklist_df.assign(done=[0, 0, 0, 0]),
    x="task",
    y="done",
    title="scANVI implementation status",
)
style_bar(checklist_plot, height=420)
```

## References Consulted

```{python}
references_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {
            "resource": "scANVI documentation",
            "role": "current recommended usage pattern",
            "link": "https://scvi-tools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/user_guide/models/scanvi.html",
        },
        {
            "resource": "scvi-tools documentation",
            "role": "framework-level requirements and positioning",
            "link": "https://docs.scvi-tools.org/en/latest/index.html",
        },
    ]
)
format_table(references_df)
```

## Session Info

```{python}
session_df = package_versions_table(["scanpy", "anndata", "pandas", "numpy", "plotly", "scvi-tools"])
format_table(session_df)
```
